lessons.title: Language Transfer · Track 1
lessons.instructions: Pause after every question and answer OUT LOUD before the student does. Never just listen.
lessons.teaches: Thousands of French words you already own: -tion/-sion words (all feminine), c’est.

lessons.title: Pronunciation 1/55 · The French alphabet for beginners
lessons.instructions: Optional now — watch when you enjoy it. Pronunciation also comes from LT (speak along!) and the app's 🔊 buttons.
lessons.teaches: One building block of the French sound system.

lessons.title: Language Transfer · Track 2
lessons.instructions: Pause after every question and answer OUT LOUD before the student does. Never just listen.
lessons.teaches: -ible/-able adjectives transfer from English; building “c’est + adjective” sentences.

lessons.title: Cartoon · Peppa Pig en français (1 episode)
lessons.instructions: Watch ONE episode. No subtitles, no pausing, no translating — the pictures carry the meaning. Confusion is normal and useful.
lessons.teaches: Your ear meeting real French rhythm at kid speed — pure listening training.

lessons.title: Pronunciation 2/55 · The French ACCENT AIGU
lessons.instructions: Optional now — watch when you enjoy it. Pronunciation also comes from LT (speak along!) and the app's 🔊 buttons.
lessons.teaches: One building block of the French sound system.

lessons.title: Language Transfer · Track 3
lessons.instructions: Pause after every question and answer OUT LOUD before the student does. Never just listen.
lessons.teaches: je veux — wanting things, and following it with a second verb (the infinitive).

lessons.title: Writing · say it in your own words
Chapter 1 checkpoint
canDoStatements: I can greet someone, thank them, and say goodbye.
I can say what I want with “je veux / je voudrais” + verb.
I can make a sentence negative with ne … pas.
I can convert English -tion words into French without thinking.

lessons.instructions: Watch ONE episode. No subtitles, no pausing, no translating — the pictures carry the meaning. Confusion is normal and useful.

canDoStatements: I finished all 40 Language Transfer tracks.
I finished the 55-lesson pronunciation course.
I can build sentences with vouloir / pouvoir / aller / être / avoir without stopping to think.
I understand the logic of French — now it’s about volume.
